intro: Adenoropium glaucum, also known as Guaco or Titi, is a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Adenoropium glaucum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adenoropium glaucum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to soothe respiratory issues and promote wound healing.
